summary: Bounded 19-prompt sample with built-in semantic search disabled measured single subprocess p50 52.5 ms, p95 58.1 ms, average 53.1 ms; batch stdin mode measured 294.4 ms total and 15.5 ms per prompt.

Modeled corpus-order TTL curve at 30-second prompt cadence: 1 min 0.0% safe hits, 5 min 4.5%, 15 min 12.0%, 30 min 15.5%, 60 min 26.0%; risky near misses increased from 7 at 5 min to 16 at 15 min and 22 at 30 min.

Recommended cache design is two-tier: 15-minute source freshness cache keyed by workspace plus advisor source signature, and 5-minute exact prompt-result cache keyed by normalized prompt fingerprint plus source signature, threshold settings, runtime, and semantic mode.

Brief-length curve: 40 tokens top-1 only is unsafe for failed recommendations; 60 tokens is minimum safe because it includes confidence and uncertainty; 80 tokens is the default because it adds freshness; 120 tokens preserves top-2 fallback for ambiguity/debug modes.

Partial when-to-fire answer: fire for prompts with at least 4 meaningful tokens or explicit command/work markers, always fire for memory-save/resume and deep-loop phrases, and skip only short casual prompts with no marker.

Similar same-skill sample pairs had average confidence about 0.948 and average absolute confidence difference 0.000, but lexical near misses across deep-research/deep-review families rule out similarity-only recommendation reuse.
